events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess missing bonding jumper around water meter. NEC 250.104 covers bonding of other metal piping systems. In residential, metal water piping may require bonding to service equipment, ground rod, etc. Missing jumper around water meter is non-compliant; water meter may interrupt electrical continuity, so bonding jumper required around meter to ensure continuity of grounding/bonding path. Risk: high? Could be shock/equipment damage if piping becomes energized. Decision fail. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value 0. Sound etc default. Findings: missing bonding jumper around water meter; discontinuity in metal water piping bonding path. Actions: install bonding jumper around water meter per NEC 250.104, ensure proper sizing and connections. Rationale. References: NEC 250.104(A)(1)/(2), 250.30 maybe separately derived systems? Actually 250.30 is grounding/bonding of separately derived systems; not directly water meter. But reference material includes 250.30. Could mention NEC 250.104 for metal water piping bonding; 250.30 for system bonding generally. Need JSON valid. Ensure no prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Bonding jumper is missing around the water meter.",
    "The water meter creates a discontinuity in the metal water piping system that can break the bonding/grounding path.",
    "If the piping becomes energized, personnel may be exposed to electric shock and equipment may be damaged."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an appropriately sized bonding jumper around the water meter to maintain electrical continuity of the metal water piping system.",
    "Terminate bonding jumper with listed connectors to clean metal surfaces on each side of the meter.",
    "Verify continuity and compliance with NEC 250.104 before closing out the work."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 250.104 requires metal water piping systems to be bonded to the grounding electrode system or service equipment. A water meter interrupts piping continuity, so a bonding jumper around the meter is required. Its absence is a code violation and a shock hazard.",
  "references": ["NEC 250.104", "NEC 250.30"]
}
